title: Decoder output-projection ranker physical wrapper
hypothesis: A concrete output-projection ranker policy wrapper can mux between serial_lpc1 and radix-4 rank-tree paths for r64, and bank both paths for r128, while preserving top-1 RTL equivalence and exposing the physical overhead of carrying both selected paths.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What is the PPA overhead of a concrete policy wrapper that contains serial and rank-tree paths plus selection mux/control for r64 and banked r128 producer tiles?
include: generated r64 policy wrapper with serial_lpc1 and rank-tree paths selected by use_ranktree
generated r128 policy wrapper with two banked r64 serial paths and two banked r64 rank-tree paths
RTL simulation of both selected paths for each wrapper
wrapper mux/control and inactive-path area overhead

expected_benefit: moves from policy contract to concrete wrapper RTL that can be synthesized
checks whether carrying both serial and rank-tree paths is physically tolerable
reveals whether path-specific wrappers are preferable to a single muxed policy wrapper
risks: r128 wrapper pin pressure may dominate the standalone physical sweep
inactive paths are present in area/power because clock gating is not modeled
the final producer-integrated wrapper may have different routing than this standalone macro-style wrapper

required_evaluations.objective: Generate concrete output-projection ranker policy wrappers for r64 and r128, simulate both selected paths, and measure wrapper PPA.

expected_result.reason: If the muxed policy wrapper PPA is acceptable, integrate it into the output-projection producer wrapper; otherwise split serial and rank-tree wrappers or add clock/path gating.
